div1.entryFree: Beard, vb., to face, to set at defiance: no man so potent breathes upon the ground but I will b. him, H4A IV, 1, 12. do what thou darest; I b. thee to thy face. What! am I dared and --ed to my face? H6A I, 3, 44. H6A I, 3, 44 brave thee! ay, and b. thee too, H6B IV, 10, 40. Used in jest by Hamlet: thy face is valanced since I saw thee last: comest thou to b. me in Denmark? Hml. II, 2, 443.
